description: Press the two green buttons on panel A
Moves to the workbench and sits down
Takes the high-voltage card
Places the card on the work area
Connects the battery to the screwdriver
Removes the workbench screws using the screwdriver
Fixes the card to the workbench using the screwdriver
Turns on the power supply via the tower switch
Adjusts the power supply current knob until the green LED turns on
Adjusts the power supply voltage knob setting it to 5 Volts
Connects the power supply cables to the card's power points
Turns off the card's power supply via the tower switch
Resets the power supply current and voltage knobs to 0
Disconnects the power supply cables
Removes the card from the workbench using the screwdriver
Turns on the soldering iron via the tower switch
Sets the soldering iron temperature to 200 degrees using the yellow UP button
Grasps the capacitor on the card with pliers
Takes the soldering iron tip
Touches the first pin of the capacitor with the tip
Touches the second pin of the capacitor with the tip
Places the pliers on the workbench
Places the soldering iron tip
Puts the card vertically
Touches the first pin of the capacitor on the back of the card with the tip
Touches the second pin of the capacitor on the back of the card with the tip
Touches the second pin of the transformer on the back of the card with the tip
Reduces the soldering iron temperature to minimum (160 degrees) using the yellow DOWN button
Turns off the soldering iron via the tower switch
Turns on the oscilloscope via the tower switch
Activates the probe channel number 2 using the blue-bordered CH2 MENU button
Connects the probe ground clip to test point number 1
Uses the probe tip to check some signal brought to test point number 2 on the card
Presses the oscilloscope's Auto Set button
Randomly rotates the position knob
Uses the probe tip at test point number 3 on the card
Uses the probe tip at test point number 4 on the card
Uses the probe tip at test point number 5 on the card
Uses the probe tip at test point number 7 on the card
Disconnects the ground clip and oscilloscope probe
Deactivates the probe channel number 2
Turns off the oscilloscope via the tower switch
Uses the probe tip at test point number 6 on the card
Reinserts the screws in the work area using the screwdriver
Disconnects the screwdriver battery and connects it to the battery charger
Turns on the screwdriver battery charger via the tower switch
Notes the test operation results in the register
Presses the two red buttons on panel A
Touches the first pin of the transformer on the back of the card with the tip
Turns off the screwdriver battery charger via the tower switch
Removes the workbench screws using the screwdriver
Fixes the card to the workbench using the screwdriver
Removes the card from the workbench using the screwdriver
Reinserts the screws in the work area using the screwdriver
Takes the low-voltage card
Grasps the resistor on the card with pliers
Touches the first pin of the resistor with the tip
Touches the second pin of the resistor with the tip
Touches the first pin of the resistor on the back of the card with the tip
Touches the second pin of the resistor on the back of the card with the tip
Unscrews the 4 screws on the back of the card using the screwdriver
Removes the display from the card
Connects the display to the card using the screwdriver
Unscrews the 4 screws on the back of the card using the screwdriver
Connects the display to the card using the screwdriver
Grasps the transformer on the card with pliers
Touches the first pin of the transformer with the tip
Touches the second pin of the transformer with the tip
